description: GA90C is a stationary, single-stage, oil-injected screw compressor driven by an electric motor, available in air-cooled and water-cooled variants with integrated electronic control and optional air dryer.

components.name: compressor_element
components.role: compresses air using twin screw rotors with oil injection for cooling and sealing
components.evidence: Air drawn through filter (AF) and open inlet valve (IV) into compressor element (E) is compressed

operating_ranges.evidence: Liquid-injected screw compressor elements can be manufactured for high pressure ratios, with one compression stage usually being sufficient for pressure up to 14 and even 17 bar
Motor shaft speed r/min: 2970
Oil capacity, GA l: 29.5
Temperature of air leaving outlet valve, approx. celsius: 30

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: The rotors and housing are manufactured with ultimate precision to minimize leakage from the pressure side to the inlet
reduced internal leakage due to asymmetric screw profiles

fault_conditions.name: inlet_valve_stuck_closed
fault_conditions.description: compressor starts running but does not load after delay time
fault_conditions.symptoms: no loading after startup
fault_conditions.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) stuck in closed position

fault_conditions.name: inlet_valve_stuck_open
fault_conditions.description: compressor does not unload
fault_conditions.symptoms: safety valve blows
fault_conditions.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) does not close

fault_conditions.name: oil_separator_clogged
fault_conditions.description: oil separator element blocked
fault_conditions.symptoms: compressor air output or pressure below normal
fault_conditions.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged

maintenance_actions.name: oil_separator_replacement
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: air flow capacity and pressure performance
maintenance_actions.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged → Have element replaced

maintenance_actions.name: separator_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: Every time the separator element is renewed, examine the discharge pipe and the inside of the oil separator vessel for carbon deposits

assumptions.statement: Oil injection provides cooling, lubrication and sealing
assumptions.reason: Manual states oil function is to cool and lubricate the compressor element's moving parts, to cool the air being compressed internally, and to reduce the return leakage to the inlet

assumptions.statement: Twin screw rotors operate without mechanical contact
assumptions.reason: Manual states the rotors neither come into contact with each other nor with the compressor housing for oil-free variants

components.name: air_receiver_oil_separator
components.role: separates oil from compressed air and provides air storage
components.evidence: Compressed air and oil flow into air receiver/oil separator (AR) via check valve (CV). The air is discharged through outlet valve (AV)

operating_ranges.evidence: Maximum (unloading) pressure bar(e) 7.5, Nominal working pressure bar(e) 7
Oil capacity, GA l 29.5
Temperature of air leaving outlet valve, approx. celsius 25-30
Oil content in air less than 3 mg/m³ at 20°C
pressure drop in the pipes does not exceed 0.1 bar between the compressor and the most remote point of consumption

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged. Have element replaced.
High-quality, mineral oil with oxidation inhibitors and anti-foam and anti-wear properties
examine the discharge pipe and the inside of the oil separator vessel for carbon deposits; if excessive, the deposits should be removed

fault_conditions.name: oil_separator_clogged
fault_conditions.description: Oil separator element becomes clogged reducing air output or pressure
fault_conditions.symptoms: Compressor air output or pressure below normal
fault_conditions.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged. Have element replaced.

fault_conditions.name: excessive_carbon_deposits
fault_conditions.description: Carbon deposits accumulate in discharge pipe and oil separator vessel
fault_conditions.evidence: examine the discharge pipe and the inside of the oil separator vessel for carbon deposits; if excessive, the deposits should be removed

maintenance_actions.name: oil_and_filter_change
maintenance_actions.evidence: Remove the oil filters (OF). Clean the filter seats on the manifold. Oil the gaskets of the new elements.

maintenance_actions.name: carbon_deposit_inspection
maintenance_actions.interval: Every time the separator element is renewed
maintenance_actions.evidence: Every time the separator element is renewed, examine the discharge pipe and the inside of the oil separator vessel for carbon deposits

maintenance_actions.name: condensate_drainage
maintenance_actions.evidence: the air receiver must be fitted with a condensate drainage device

assumptions.statement: Oil separator operates as integral part of air receiver system
assumptions.reason: Manual describes air receiver and oil separation functions together in compressed air treatment context

assumptions.statement: Operating pressures apply to complete air receiver oil separator assembly
assumptions.reason: Pressure specifications given for overall compressor system including air receiver and separation components

components.name: drive_motor
components.role: provides mechanical power to drive the compressor element
components.evidence: GA are stationary, single-stage, oil-injected screw compressors driven by an electric motor

operating_ranges.evidence: Motor shaft speed r/min: 2970 (50Hz), 3575 (60Hz)
Power input, GA kW: 98.7-105.7 depending on pressure and frequency
Overload relay F21 settings up to 190A for various voltage configurations
Supply voltage ranges from 220-230V to 575V across different configurations
Frequency specifications show 50Hz and 60Hz operation

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Bearings must be regreased every 4,000 operating hours

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: service life shortened by half for every 10°C above limit
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: If the upper limit is exceeded by 10°C over a sustained period of time, the service life of the insulation is shortened by about half

fault_conditions.name: motor_overload
fault_conditions.description: Drive motor overload condition triggers shutdown
fault_conditions.symptoms: Compressor shutdown with overload indication
fault_conditions.evidence: Drive motor (M1-Fig. 1.3) overload triggers shut-down

fault_conditions.name: incorrect_rotation
fault_conditions.description: Motor rotating in wrong direction
fault_conditions.symptoms: Improper compressor operation or cooling fan issues
fault_conditions.evidence: Check the operation of the cooling fan when rotating anticlockwise

maintenance_actions.name: bearing_regreasing
maintenance_actions.evidence: Bearings must be regreased every 4,000 operating hours

assumptions.statement: Motor operates in star-delta starting configuration
assumptions.reason: Multiple references to star and delta contactors (K22, K23) and starting sequences in electrical diagrams

assumptions.statement: Motor provides constant speed operation at rated frequency
assumptions.reason: Specified shaft speeds are fixed values (2970 r/min at 50Hz, 3575 r/min at 60Hz) with no mention of variable speed control for this basic model

components.name: inlet_valve
components.role: controls air intake and enables load/unload operation
components.evidence: Loading plunger (LP) moves downwards → inlet valve (IV) opens fully

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) stuck in closed position or does not close/fully open

fault_conditions.description: Inlet valve stuck in closed position preventing compressor loading
fault_conditions.symptoms: Compressor starts running but does not load after delay time
fault_conditions.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) stuck in closed position

fault_conditions.name: inlet_valve_does_not_close
fault_conditions.description: Inlet valve fails to close during unload operation
fault_conditions.symptoms: Compressor does not unload, safety valve blows
fault_conditions.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) does not close

fault_conditions.name: inlet_valve_does_not_fully_open
fault_conditions.description: Inlet valve fails to open completely during load operation
fault_conditions.symptoms: Compressor air output or pressure below normal
fault_conditions.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) does not fully open

fault_conditions.name: inlet_valve_malfunction_safety_valve_blow
fault_conditions.description: Inlet valve malfunctioning causes safety valve to blow after loading
fault_conditions.symptoms: Safety valve (SV) blows after loading
fault_conditions.evidence: Inlet valve (IV) malfunctioning

maintenance_actions.name: inlet_valve_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: Have valve checked

assumptions.statement: Inlet valve operates in binary mode (fully open or fully closed)
assumptions.reason: Manual describes load/unload operation where valve is either fully opened (loaded) or fully closed (unloaded) with no intermediate position

assumptions.statement: Inlet valve is controlled by solenoid valve Y1
assumptions.reason: Multiple fault conditions link solenoid valve Y1 malfunctions to inlet valve operation issues

components.name: air_filter
components.role: filters incoming air to remove contaminants
components.evidence: Air drawn through filter (AF) and open inlet valve (IV) into compressor element (E)

operating_ranges.evidence: Maximum air inlet temperature: 40 celsius, Minimum air inlet temperature: 0 celsius
Air inlet pressure (absolute): 1 bar
Relative humidity: 0%

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Choked air inlet filter element (AF)
More than 80% of particles contaminating compressed air are smaller than 2 µm. Can easily pass through compressor's inlet filter

fault_conditions.name: choked_filter
fault_conditions.description: air inlet filter element becomes clogged
fault_conditions.symptoms: compressor air output or pressure below normal
fault_conditions.evidence: Choked air inlet filter element (AF). → Replace filter element.

maintenance_actions.name: filter_replacement
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: filter element condition and service indicator
maintenance_actions.evidence: Stop the compressor. Switch off the voltage. Release the snap clips and remove the dust trap and air filter element. Clean the trap. Discard the filter element. Refit the new element and the trap. Reset the service indicator

maintenance_actions.name: pre_filter_installation
maintenance_actions.evidence: A pre-filter (cyclone, panel or rotary band filter) should be used on installations where the surrounding air has a high dust concentration

assumptions.statement: Air filter operates at reference conditions of 20°C, 1 bar absolute pressure, and 0% relative humidity
assumptions.reason: These are the standard reference conditions specified for compressor operation

assumptions.statement: Filter requires replacement when choked rather than cleaning
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies to discard the filter element and refit new element during maintenance

components.name: oil_cooler
components.role: cools oil to maintain proper operating temperature
components.evidence: By-pass valve (BV) operation: Below 40 degrees celsius: BV shuts off oil supply from oil cooler (Co)

operating_ranges.evidence: At water inlet temperature below 25 degrees celsius and temperature rise = 15 degrees celsius
At water inlet temperature between 25 and 40 degrees celsius and temperature rise = 10 degrees celsius
At water inlet temperature below 25 degrees celsius
The cooling water from a water-cooled compressor with a temperature up to 90°C
Cooling water consumption 3) l/min 95
Cooling water consumption 4) l/min 142

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Chloride and sulphate are interactive. In pass-through systems the sum of the squares must not exceed 85,000
Suspended solids (as SiO₂) < 10 mg/l, Organics (KMnO₄ Consumption) < 25 mg/l

maintenance_actions.name: cooling_water_flow_regulation

maintenance_actions.evidence: Regulate during loaded running to obtain the most suitable temperature at the outlet of the compressor element

assumptions.reason: Manual discusses cooling water requirements and water-cooled system specifications but does not explicitly separate oil cooler from overall cooling system

assumptions.statement: Cooling water consumption values apply to entire cooling system including oil cooler
assumptions.reason: Manual provides cooling water consumption rates but does not break down consumption by individual cooling components

components.name: air_cooler

maintenance_actions.name: cooler_cleaning
maintenance_actions.evidence: Remove any dirt from the coolers with a fibre brush. Never use a wire brush or metal objects. Then clean by air jet in reverse direction of normal flow

operating_ranges.evidence: prevents receiver pressure from dropping below minimum pressure

Maintains net pressure between programmable limits - Automatically loads and unloads compressor - Programmable settings include: Loading pressures

flow_paths.evidence: Air drawn through filter (AF) and open inlet valve (IV) into compressor element (E) is compressed. Compressed air and oil flow into air receiver/oil separator (AR) via check valve (CV). The air is discharged through outlet valve (AV) via minimum pressure valve (Vp), air cooler (Ca) and condensate trap (MTa)
By-pass valve (BV) operation: Below 40 degrees celsius: BV shuts off oil supply from oil cooler (Co). At approximately 55 degrees celsius: all oil flows through oil cooler
Control pressure fed from air receiver (AR) via solenoid valve (Y1) to loading plunger (LP) and unloading valve (UV)

operation_sequence.description: Start-up: Drive motor starts and runs in star configuration for ten seconds
operation_sequence.evidence: Ten seconds after starting: Drive motor switches over from star to delta

operation_sequence.description: Motor switches to delta configuration and compressor begins loading
operation_sequence.evidence: At the same time: Compressor starts running loaded

operation_sequence.description: Air is drawn through filter and inlet valve into compressor element
operation_sequence.evidence: Air drawn through filter (AF) and open inlet valve (IV) into compressor element (E) is compressed

operation_sequence.description: Compressed air and oil mixture flows to air receiver/oil separator
operation_sequence.evidence: Compressed air and oil flow into air receiver/oil separator (AR) via check valve (CV)

operation_sequence.description: Oil is separated and air flows through cooling and condensate removal
operation_sequence.evidence: The air is discharged through outlet valve (AV) via minimum pressure valve (Vp), air cooler (Ca) and condensate trap (MTa)

operation_sequence.description: When pressure reaches unloading setpoint, solenoid valve de-energizes and inlet valve closes
operation_sequence.evidence: When net pressure reaches unloading pressure: Solenoid valve (Y1) is de-energized. Loading plunger (LP) moves upwards → inlet valve (IV) closes air inlet opening

operation_sequence.description: When pressure drops to loading setpoint, solenoid valve energizes and inlet valve opens
operation_sequence.evidence: When loading pressure reached: Solenoid valve (Y1) is energized. Loading plunger (LP) moves downwards → inlet valve (IV) opens fully

assumptions.statement: Oil capacity is 29.5 liters for all pressure variants
assumptions.reason: Specific oil capacity values only provided in specification tables, not in operational descriptions

assumptions.statement: Motor shaft speed is 2970 rpm for 50Hz and 3575 rpm for 60Hz variants
assumptions.reason: Speed values only found in specification tables, not in operational flow descriptions

assumptions.statement: Temperature sensors provide feedback to the Elektronikon regulator
assumptions.reason: Temperature monitoring mentioned but specific signal paths not detailed in operational descriptions

operating_ranges.evidence: Liquid-injected screw compressor elements can be manufactured for high pressure ratios, with one compression stage usually being sufficient for pressure up to 14 and even 17 bar
Motor shaft speed r/min: 2970
Oil capacity, GA l: 29.5
Temperature of air leaving outlet valve, approx. celsius: 30

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: The rotors and housing are manufactured with ultimate precision to minimize leakage from the pressure side to the inlet
reduced internal leakage due to asymmetric screw profiles

maintenance_actions.name: filter_replacement

maintenance_actions.evidence: Stop the compressor. Switch off the voltage. Release the snap clips and remove the dust trap and air filter element. Clean the trap. Discard the filter element. Refit the new element and the trap. Reset the service indicator
